sourceNote: AFM-only training transcription from Viking Air PSM 1-63-1A Revision 53, Section 3.11.2, pages 3-36 to 3-37. The memory list contains only the first three actions printed in bold. The subsequent troubleshooting checks the affected BETA light and autofeather selection before directing an engine shutdown. Crew-role fields are trainer UI assignments only; they are not approved PF/PM callouts or operator SOP. Verify applicability against the aircraft-specific AFM, amendments, supplements, and operator procedures.

memory.action: Advance the unaffected engine power lever to the torque, T5, or NG limit, whichever is reached first; ensure its PROP lever is at MAX RPM (96% NP).
Aircraft control — Adjust pitch attitude to maintain altitude; maintain directional control with rudder.
Power lever of affected engine — IDLE.

flow.action: Trim aircraft as required. Flaps may remain retracted or be extended to 10° if necessary.
Compute and, if necessary, set Maximum Continuous Power on the operating engine.
Check the BETA light for the affected engine. If illuminated, complete the Steady Beta Light checklist, AFM 3.11.5.
If the autofeather system is selected ON, as shown by the green autofeather SELECT light, turn the autofeather system OFF.
If neither action resolves the problem, shut down the affected engine using Engine Shutdown in Flight, AFM 3.10.1.
